zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Trenowith於1400年9月30日出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾馬貝時，她的父親Ralph Trenoweth36歲，母親Jane Basset28歲。她於1414年與John Petit在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1471年5月4日在英國英格蘭可瓦爾費爾享年70歲，埋葬在英國可瓦爾費爾。

nameMeanings.name: Trenowith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Cornish: locative name from one or more of at least twenty places in Cornwall named with Cornish tre ‘house, settlement’ + nowyth ‘new’, with the spellings Trenewth, Trenouth, Trenowah, Trenower, Trenoweth, Trenowth, Trenute, or Trenuth.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Trenowith was born on 30 September 1400, in Mabe, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, her father, Ralph Trenoweth, was 36 and her mother, Jane Basset, was 28. She married John Petit in 1414, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 4 May 1471, in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Philleigh, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Trenowith est née le 30 septembre 1400, à Mabe, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ralph Trenoweth, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Jane Basset, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé John Petit en 1414, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 mai 1471, à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Trenowith am 30. September 1400 in Mabe, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ralph Trenoweth 36 und ihre Mutter Jane Basset 28. Sie hat 1414 in Cornwall, England John Petit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. Mai 1471 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Philleigh, Cornwall, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Trenowithが1400年9月30日にイギリスイギリスコルンワルマベで生まれた時，父Ralph Trenowethは36歳，母Jane Bassetは28歳であった。1414年にイギリスコルンワルでJohn Petitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1471年5月4日にイギリスイギリスコルンワルフィレイグで70歳で死去し，イギリスコルンワルフィレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Trenowith(이)가 1400년 9월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 메이브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph Trenoweth(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Basset(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1414년 영국 코른월에서 John Petit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1471년 5월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 필리에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 코른월 필리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Trenowith родилась 30 сентября 1400 г. в местности маб, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Ralph Trenoweth было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Basset, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Petit в 1414 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 4 мая 1471 г. в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия.
